title: OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for III.A.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that TSA SD III.A (Cybersecurity plan) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.16.13: OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.16.13' running on pan:traffic, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, nozomi:alert, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
spl:
index=ot sourcetype=nozomi:alert earliest=-7d severity>="high"
| eval uc_tag="22.16.13"
| eval nist_cat=case(match(name,"(?i)malware"),"DE.CM", match(name,"(?i)lateral"),"DE.AE", 1=1,"PR.DS")
| stats count by nist_cat, site_name
description: Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category".
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for TSA SD compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot and sourcetypes nozomi:alert. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=ot`.
visualization: Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value. Key fields: `nist_cat`, `site_name`, `uc_tag`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.signature, IDS_Attacks.src, IDS_Attacks.dest | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Incidents during planned exercises, controlled tabletop drills, or vendor maintenance windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype=nozomi:alert earliest=-7d severity>="high"
| eval uc_tag="22.16.13"
| eval nist_cat=case(match(name,"(?i)malware"),"DE.CM", match(name,"(?i)lateral"),"DE.AE", 1=1,"PR.DS")
| stats count by nist_cat, site_name
```

Understanding this SPL

**OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category (TSA incident response)** — Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category".

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: nozomi:alert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype=nozomi:alert, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **nist_cat** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by nist_cat, site_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.signature, IDS_Attacks.src, IDS_Attacks.dest | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category (TSA incident response)** — Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category".

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value
grandmaExplanation: We support pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "OT alert volume by NIST CSF-style category". That lines up with pipeline security expectations and helps the team show its work to regulators and leadership.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Intrusion_Detection data model (Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks).
